title: Access the RMS Web Application to Perform Robot Selection Actions
summary: This source describes how support personnel can reach the Geek Plus RMS or related web application view used for Robot Selection actions. Local access is available from a device connected to the OT network using the site IP address. Remote access requires authorization to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler before using the site address or RMS address. The source also notes that local teams can use shop-floor HMI stations where this view is displayed.
when_to_use: Use this reference when support personnel need to reach the RMS or web application view used for Robot Selection add or remove actions, either from an OT-network-connected device, an authorized remote connection, or a local shop-floor HMI station.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a credential or authentication procedure; the source does not provide usernames, passwords, or additional login steps.
Do not use this runbook as the procedure for actually adding or removing a robot; this source-specific runbook only covers how to access the application view used for those actions.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Determine whether access will be local or remote and use the supported access path described in the source.
Use local shop-floor HMI stations when local team access is needed, as noted by the source.

safety_notes: Use only authorized remote access through the UPS VPN and Z Scaler when remote access is required.
Do not invent passwords or additional authentication steps; the source does not provide them.
access_or_tools_needed: OT-network-connected device for local access or authorized remote access through UPS VPN and Z Scaler
IP address or RMS address for the site
Physical HMI station if using shop-floor access

access_required: Connection to the OT network for local device access
Authorization to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler for remote access
system_preconditions: A site IP address or RMS address is available for the target site
A shop-floor HMI station is available if local station access is needed

visual_references.description: Robot Selection web application view showing Add Robot and Remove Robot actions; useful as a visual confirmation that the correct RMS screen has been reached.
Training frame introducing RMS as the robot management system and describing access through a unique IP address.

steps.title: Determine the access path
steps.purpose: Identify whether the application will be reached locally on the OT network or remotely through an authorized connection.
steps.instruction: Determine whether access will be local on the OT network or remote.
steps.expected_result: A valid access path is selected before attempting to open the application.
steps.healthy_condition: The user knows whether they are using an OT-network-connected device, an authorized remote connection, or a local HMI station.
steps.failure_condition: The access path is unclear or the user attempts remote access without confirming authorization requirements.

actions.label: Identify access mode
actions.exact_action: Determine whether access will be local on the OT network or remote.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if remote access is required but authorization status is unknown.

source_evidence.text: Determine whether access will be local on the OT network or remote.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Any device connected on the OT network, whether a laptop or computer, will have access to the web software application through that IP.
Remote support will not have access to OT net unless authorized to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler; after authorization, the site address or RMS address can be used to access Geek Plus RMS.

steps.title: Access the application locally from the OT network
steps.purpose: Reach the RMS or web software application directly from a device already connected to the OT network.
steps.instruction: For local access, use a laptop, computer, or other device connected to the OT network and open the web software application through its IP address as described in the source.
steps.expected_result: The RMS or web application opens from the OT-network-connected device.
steps.healthy_condition: The application is reachable through the site IP address from the OT-network-connected device.
steps.failure_condition: The application does not open from the OT-network-connected device or the IP address is not available.

actions.label: Open local RMS application
actions.exact_action: Use a laptop, computer, or other device connected to the OT network and open the web software application through its IP address.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: RMS described as a web application accessed through a unique IP address.
Robot Selection interface that can confirm the correct application view has been reached.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the device is not connected to the OT network.
Stop and escalate if the site IP address is not available from the source-supported access path.

source_evidence.text: For local access, use a laptop, computer, or other device connected to the OT network and open the web software application through its IP address as described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Any device connected on the OT network, whether a laptop or computer, will have access to the web software application through that IP.
Users access the RMS web application through a unique IP address with username and password.

steps.title: Confirm remote authorization requirements
steps.purpose: Ensure remote access is attempted only through the authorized path described by the source.
steps.instruction: For remote access, confirm authorization to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler before attempting to reach the OT-network-hosted application.
steps.expected_result: Remote authorization status is confirmed before access is attempted.
steps.healthy_condition: The user is authorized to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler.
steps.failure_condition: The user is not authorized to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler.

actions.label: Verify remote authorization
actions.exact_action: Confirm authorization to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler before attempting to reach the OT-network-hosted application.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if remote access is needed but UPS VPN or Z Scaler authorization is not available.

source_evidence.text: For remote access, confirm authorization to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler before attempting to reach the OT-network-hosted application.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Remote support will not have access to OT net unless authorized to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler; after authorization, the site address or RMS address can be used to access Geek Plus RMS.

steps.title: Open Geek Plus RMS remotely
steps.purpose: Reach the site RMS after remote authorization is in place.
steps.instruction: After remote authorization is available, enter the address or RMS address for the particular site to access Geek Plus RMS.
steps.expected_result: Geek Plus RMS opens for the selected site through the authorized remote path.
steps.healthy_condition: The site address or RMS address successfully reaches Geek Plus RMS.
steps.failure_condition: The site address or RMS address does not open Geek Plus RMS after authorization is confirmed.

actions.label: Enter site RMS address
actions.exact_action: Enter the address or RMS address for the particular site to access Geek Plus RMS.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection screen in Geek Plus RMS as a confirmation that the correct site application has been reached.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if authorization is confirmed but the site address or RMS address still does not provide access.

source_evidence.text: After remote authorization is available, enter the address or RMS address for the particular site to access Geek Plus RMS.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Remote support will not have access to OT net unless authorized to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler; after authorization, the site address or RMS address can be used to access Geek Plus RMS.

steps.title: Use a local HMI station when shop-floor access is needed
steps.purpose: Provide a local access option when the view is needed on the shop floor.
steps.instruction: If local shop-floor access is needed, use one of the physical HMI stations where this view is displayed.
steps.expected_result: The user reaches the RMS view from a shop-floor HMI station.
steps.healthy_condition: An HMI station is available and displays the needed RMS view for the local team.
steps.failure_condition: No HMI station is available or the needed view is not displayed.

actions.label: Go to HMI station
actions.exact_action: Use one of the physical HMI stations where this view is displayed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection display that the source says is shown on local HMI stations.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if local shop-floor access is needed but no HMI station is available.

source_evidence.text: If local shop-floor access is needed, use one of the physical HMI stations where this view is displayed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The shop floor has two HMI stations where this view is displayed for the local team, with one located at the hospital.

success_criteria: The user reaches the RMS or web application view needed to perform Robot Selection add or remove actions.
The correct site application is accessible through a supported local, remote, or HMI-based path described by the source.
healthy_conditions: An OT-network-connected device can reach the web software application through the site IP address.
Authorized remote support can use the site address or RMS address to access Geek Plus RMS.
A shop-floor HMI station displays the needed view for the local team.
failure_conditions: Remote access is needed but UPS VPN or Z Scaler authorization is not available.
The site IP address or RMS address is not available or does not provide access.
The needed local HMI station is not available.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if remote access is needed but UPS VPN or Z Scaler authorization is not available.
Escalate if the source-supported local or remote address does not provide access after the required access path is confirmed.
Escalate if local shop-floor access is required but the HMI station is unavailable.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection interface with Add Robot and Remove Robot actions, used as a visual confirmation of the target RMS view.
RMS web application overview and note that access is through a unique IP address.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Any device connected on the OT network, whether a laptop or computer, will have access to the web software application through that IP.
Remote support will not have access to OT net unless authorized to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler; after authorization, the site address or RMS address can be used to access Geek Plus RMS.
The shop floor has two HMI stations where this view is displayed for the local team, with one located at the hospital.

missing_details: The source does not provide the actual site IP address or RMS address values.
The source does not provide usernames, passwords, or detailed authentication steps.
The source does not provide a screenshot or artifact of the physical HMI station itself; only the RMS/Robot Selection screen is available as visual support.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
